title: Shared decoration lifecycle and reveal-state gate
summary: util.on_decorate owns normalized mode, cursor, edit, and viewport lifecycle handling for participating frontend renderers.
details: ModeChanged is mandatory and added centrally even if a module's registration list omits it.
The lifecycle stores the prior reveal set and diffs it against the new reveal set on cursor or mode transitions.
Renderers with a row callback repaint only rows whose revealed/decorated state changed; other renderers receive a full visible-range refresh.
Paint signatures deduplicate equivalent full passes and include content tick, cursor, viewport, mode, diagnostics generation, recording state, and cold-open state.
WinScrolled is debounced into the DansViewportSettled User event so scroll bursts cause one repaint.
rationale: Reveal behavior is a cross-cutting presentation contract. Letting each feature choose its own transition events caused feature-specific stale rows.
Reveal-set diffs preserve the existing cursor-motion performance optimization while extending it correctly to Visual selections.

provenance.note: Compared every setup registration and reproduced stale aliases after Visual exit.
Current implementation centralizes mandatory ModeChanged subscription and reveal delta calculation.

evidence.claim: Fifty interaction assertions cover visual modes and representative frontend modules.
Incremental row repaint matches a full refresh for cursor reveal transitions.

questions: Where are frontend mode transitions coordinated?
How are only changed reveal rows repainted?
Why must modules not independently choose whether ModeChanged matters?

last_verified.method: Source audit, real mode-transition regression, and complete headless suite.
